seo.title: AI Agent Testing Strategies in Semantic Kernel Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent testing strategies in Semantic Kernel agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Testing Strategies challenges in Semantic Kernel arise from complex interactions between plugins, planners, and memory components, leading to unreliable agent behavior in enterprise applications. Issues like plugin chaining errors, invalid plans, memory hallucinations, and data contamination undermine agent reliability during multi-step task execution. This guide explores how these manifest specifically in Semantic Kernel's patterns and APIs, with targeted detection and remediation strategies.

howItManifests.symptom: Sequential plugin calls fail when upstream plugin returns malformed data that downstream plugins can't parse
howItManifests.impact: Complete task failure with untraceable error propagation through KernelFunction invocation chain

howItManifests.symptom: SequentialPlanner or HandlebarsPlanner creates plans calling non-existent functions or wrong parameter types
howItManifests.impact: Agent stuck in infinite planning loops or runtime FunctionInvocationException errors

howItManifests.symptom: VolatileMemoryStore or ChatMemoryHistory retrieves fabricated conversation context
howItManifests.impact: Agent responds with incorrect information based on hallucinated chat history

howItManifests.symptom: Shared ContextVariables between plugins cause state leakage across independent function calls
howItManifests.impact: Incorrect plugin outputs due to residual data from previous executions polluting current Context

detection.name: Plugin Chain Error Tracer
detection.description: Detects cascading failures by monitoring KernelFunction invocation stack and input/output validation

detection.name: Plan Validation Monitor
detection.description: Validates planner output against available functions before execution

detection.name: Memory Hallucination Detector
detection.description: Compares memory recall against conversation history for factual consistency
detection.code:
```python
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory
from semantic_kernel.contents import ChatHistory

def detect_memory_hallucination(history: ChatHistory, recalled: str):
    # Extract actual conversation facts
    actual_facts = [msg.content for msg in history if msg.role == 'user']
    
    hallucinated = False
    for fact in actual_facts:
        if fact.lower() not in recalled.lower():
            hallucinated = True
            print(f'🚨 MEMORY HALLUCINATION: Expected {fact}, got {recalled}')
    
    return hallucinated
```

fixes.name: Input Validation Middleware
fixes.description: Adds schema validation to all plugin inputs to prevent cascading failures

fixes.name: Safe Planner with Fallback
fixes.description: Implements retry logic and fallback plans for planner failures

fixes.name: Isolated Context Execution
fixes.description: Creates fresh Context for each plugin to prevent data contamination
fixes.code:
```python
from semantic_kernel import ContextVariables

def isolated_plugin_call(kernel, function_name: str, input_data: dict):
    # Fresh context per call - no contamination
    clean_context = ContextVariables()
    for key, value in input_data.items():
        clean_context[key] = value
    
    result = kernel.invoke(function_name, clean_context)
    return result

# Usage
result1 = isolated_plugin_call(kernel, 'plugin_a', {'data': 'clean'})
result2 = isolated_plugin_call(kernel, 'plugin_b', {'data': 'isolated'})  # No contamination
```

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Testing Strategies Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as a Kernel filter to automatically detect all four AI Agent failure modes in Semantic Kernel applications. By monitoring function results, plan execution, memory retrieval, and context state, Vex provides real-time alerts and automated remediation workflows. The Vex SDK hooks into Semantic Kernel's filter pipeline, enabling comprehensive observability across plugin chains, planner decisions, and memory operations without code changes.

Vex's AI-powered analysis identifies cascading errors before they propagate, validates plans against function registries, detects memory hallucinations through semantic similarity scoring, and traces cross-plugin contamination patterns. Integration takes minutes via pip install vex-sdk, with automatic dashboard setup and remediation recommendations. Teams gain instant visibility into agent reliability metrics and can enforce testing thresholds across deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
```python
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from vex_sdk import VexMonitor

# Install: pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk

kernel = Kernel()

# Vex auto-detects all 4 failure modes
vex = VexMonitor(
    project_id="your-project",
    monitor_name="semantic-kernel-agent-testing"
)

# Single line integration as result filter
kernel.filters.add_result_filter(vex.filter_handler)

# Vex now monitors:
# ✅ Plugin chaining errors
# ✅ Invalid execution plans
# ✅ Memory hallucinations
# ✅ Cross-plugin contamination

# Run your agent normally
result = await kernel.invoke("planner", "complex task")

# Vex dashboard shows issues + auto-fixes
print(vex.get_issues())  # ['cascading_error', 'memory_hallucination']
```
